steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the underlined phrase "known collectively as the silversword alliance" does in the sentence. Read it precisely: it follows "a family of plants" and gives that family a name. The function is labeling — supplying the name used to identify the group.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It lists species from a family of plants found only on the Hawaiian Islands.: The phrase names one group, it does not list multiple species. Listing means enumeration; a single collective name is not a list. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains why *Dubautia raillardioides* is unique among members of the silversword alliance.: The phrase introduces the alliance name; it says nothing about what makes Dubautia raillardioides unique — that topic comes later in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — It supplies the name used to refer to a group of related plant species.: "Known collectively as the silversword alliance" does exactly this — it gives the collective label for the family. ✓
  • Choice D — It provides the common name for the *Dubautia raillardioides* plant.: "Silversword alliance" names the entire plant family, not just Dubautia raillardioides. Confuses the species with the group. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
